questions.question: What is the topic for Week 32?
questions.explanation: The topic explicitly listed for Lesson Thirty-Two is LIVE HEALTHY.

questions.question: Which of the following was the topic for Week 31?
questions.explanation: Self-Worth was the topic for Week 31.

questions.question: For bodily exercise profiteth little: but ________ is profitable unto all things...
questions.explanation: The memory verse (1 Timothy 4:8) says 'but godliness is profitable unto all things...'

questions.question: Provide the book, chapter, and verse for this week's memory verse.
questions.explanation: The memory verse reference is 1 Timothy 4:8.

questions.question: Type out the memory verse for Week 32 in full (KJV/NKJV).
questions.answer: For bodily exercise profiteth little: but godliness is profitable unto all things, having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.
questions.explanation: Full text recall of 1 Timothy 4:8.

questions.question: According to the memory verse (1 Timothy 4:8), godliness has the promise of the life that now is, and of what else?
options: B. That which is to come
questions.explanation: The verse says 'having promise of the life that now is, and of that which is to come.'

questions.question: According to the introduction, we should pay serious attention to career and family but it is okay to neglect our health.
questions.explanation: The introduction explicitly states 'we should not neglect our health (Acts 27:34; 1 Timothy 5:23).'

questions.question: What is the heading of the first outline?
questions.explanation: Outline I is titled 'THE IMPORTANCE'.

questions.question: According to Outline I, why is it our responsibility to prioritise our health?
options: A. Because medical bills are high
B. Because our bodies are God's temple and the containers that enable us to fulfill God's purpose
C. So we can look better than others
questions.explanation: The text says '...because our bodies are God’s temple (1 Corinthians 6:19) and the containers that enable us to exist and interact with the physical world without which we would be unable to fulfil God’s purpose...'

questions.question: Which of these is listed as a reason why it is important to take care of our bodies?
options: A. Good health makes us wealthy immediately
C. It guarantees a life without problems
D. It removes the need for prayer
questions.explanation: Point 5 in Outline I says 'Living healthy increases our self-worth (Daniel 1:12-15).'

questions.question: According to the Week 32 lesson, good health is more expensive than ill-health.
questions.explanation: Point 6 says 'It is less expensive than ill-health (Mark 5:25-26).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that good health is God's will for us?
questions.explanation: Point 1 cites '(Jeremiah 30:17)'.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to support that it is impossible to serve God without good health?
questions.explanation: Point 2 cites '(Mark 5:18-20)'.

questions.question: What is the heading of the second outline?
questions.explanation: Outline II is titled 'THE TIPS'.

questions.question: What does Outline II say we must minimize the intake of?
questions.explanation: The text states 'Practically, we must minimise the intake of sugar and processed food.'

questions.question: According to the Week 32 lesson, negative thoughts and mind-sets can be detrimental to our health.
questions.explanation: The text says 'It is important to renew our minds because negative thoughts and mind-sets can be detrimental to our health (Romans 12:2).'

questions.question: Why does the Week 32 lesson suggest spending time outdoors and reducing screen time?
options: C. To prevent eye problems and brain malfunction
questions.explanation: The text states 'Spend time outdoors and reduce screen time to prevent eye problems and brain malfunction.'

questions.question: What happens when you steer clear of alcoholic/strong drinks, according to the Week 32 lesson?
options: B. You avoid damaging the organs and the image of the victims
questions.explanation: The text says 'Steer clear of alcoholic/strong drinks: it damages the organs and the image of the victims (Proverbs 20:1; 31:6).'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding regular exercise aiding blood circulation?
questions.explanation: The text says 'Engage in regular exercise to aid blood circulation in the body (1Timothy. 4:8a).'

questions.question: According to the conclusion, what we eat or drink is our first medicine.
questions.explanation: The conclusion explicitly states 'What we eat or drink is our first medicine.'

questions.question: Where is this verse found? 'Please test your servants for ten days, and let them give us vegetables to eat and water to drink.'
questions.verse_text: Please test your servants for ten days, and let them give us vegetables to eat and water to drink.
questions.explanation: This is verse 12 from the main Bible Passage.

questions.question: According to the Bible Passage (Daniel 1:13), what did Daniel ask to be examined after the ten days?
options: A. Their physical strength in battle
C. Their appearance and the appearance of those who ate the king's delicacies
D. Their ability to interpret dreams
questions.explanation: Verse 13 says 'Then let our appearance be examined before you, and the appearance of the young men who eat the portion of the king’s delicacies...'

questions.question: According to Daniel 1:15, at the end of ten days, how did Daniel and his friends appear compared to the young men who ate the king's delicacies?
options: C. Better and fatter in flesh
questions.explanation: Verse 15 says '...their features appeared better and fatter in flesh than all the young men...'

questions.question: First and foremost, we need to involve ________ in our desire to be healthy.
questions.explanation: Outline II begins 'First and foremost, we need to involve God in our desire to be healthy.'

questions.question: Which scripture is cited to show that good health increases our productivity?
questions.explanation: Point 4 cites '(Proverbs 31:17)' for increasing productivity.

questions.question: Sitting for long periods at a stretch is detrimental to our health, so we must ensure that we walk more.
questions.explanation: Outline II states 'We must ensure that we walk more than sit in a position for long periods at a stretch as this is detrimental to our health.'

questions.question: Which verse is cited to remind us that our bodies are God's temple?
questions.explanation: Outline I cites '(1 Corinthians 6:19)' for bodies being God's temple.

questions.question: Which scripture is cited regarding being mindful of what you consume?
questions.explanation: Outline II cites '(Proverbs 23:1-3, 20-21; 1 Corinthians 10:31)' next to 'Be mindful of what you consume'.

questions.question: It is our responsibility and our offering of gratitude to God to prioritise our health because our bodies are the ________ that enable us to exist and interact with the physical world.
questions.explanation: Outline I says 'and the containers that enable us to exist and interact with the physical world...'

questions.question: Which of these is a reason listed in Outline I for taking care of our bodies?
options: A. It brings glory to God
B. It guarantees a wealthy life
D. It makes us smarter than others
questions.explanation: Point 3 in Outline I says 'Good health brings glory to God (Acts 3:8-10).'
